questions.question: What is reflection of light?
questions.answer: Bouncing back of light rays from a surface into the same medium.

questions.question: State the first law of reflection.
questions.answer: Angle of incidence equals angle of reflection.

questions.question: State the second law of reflection.
questions.answer: Incident ray, reflected ray and normal lie in the same plane.

questions.question: What type of image is formed by a plane mirror?
questions.answer: Virtual, erect and laterally inverted image.

questions.question: What is lateral inversion?
questions.answer: Left side of object appears as right side in the mirror image.

questions.question: What is the focal length of a spherical mirror?
questions.answer: Half the radius of curvature (f = R/2).

questions.question: Which mirror is used as a rear-view mirror in vehicles?
questions.answer: Convex mirror.

questions.question: What is the nature of image formed by a convex mirror?
questions.answer: Virtual, erect and diminished.

questions.question: What is refraction of light?
questions.answer: Bending of light when it passes from one medium to another.

questions.question: State Snell’s law.
questions.answer: sin i / sin r = constant (refractive index).

questions.question: What is refractive index?
questions.answer: Ratio of speed of light in vacuum to speed in the medium (n = c/v).

questions.question: Why does a pencil appear bent in water?
questions.answer: Due to refraction of light at water-air interface.

questions.question: What is apparent depth?
questions.answer: The depth at which an object in water appears to be when viewed from air.

questions.question: What is a convex lens?
questions.answer: Lens thicker at centre and thinner at edges that converges light rays.

questions.question: What is a concave lens?
questions.answer: Lens thinner at centre and thicker at edges that diverges light rays.

questions.question: What is the power of a lens?
questions.answer: Reciprocal of focal length in metres (P = 1/f).

questions.question: What is the SI unit of power of a lens?
questions.answer: Dioptre (D).

questions.question: State the lens formula.
What is magnification?
questions.answer: Ratio of image height to object height (m = h'/h = v/u).

questions.question: Why do we see a rainbow after rain?
questions.answer: Due to refraction, dispersion and total internal reflection in water droplets.

questions.question: What causes twinkling of stars?
questions.answer: Atmospheric refraction due to varying density of air layers.

questions.question: Why do convex lenses converge light rays?
questions.answer: Because they are thicker at the centre, causing rays to bend inwards.

questions.question: Why is a concave mirror used in solar cookers?
questions.answer: It converges sunlight to a point, producing high temperature.

questions.question: What happens when light goes from denser to rarer medium?
questions.answer: It bends away from the normal.

questions.question: What is total internal reflection?
questions.answer: Complete reflection of light inside denser medium when angle > critical angle.
